stem: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. The alternate exterior angles are $(4x + 7)°$ and $(6x - 33)°$. A second transversal creates a corresponding angle of $(3x + 22)°$ with the first parallel line. What is the measure of this corresponding angle?
explanation: Alternate exterior angles are equal: $4x+7 = 6x-33$. So $40 = 2x$, $x = 20$. The corresponding angle is $3(20)+22 = 82°$.

distractorRationale.B: Computed $4(20) + 7 = 87°$ (the alternate exterior angle, not what was asked).
